rulings.Case Title: JAMES ICARD et al VS. ECOLAB, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order Regarding The Pltf'S Oct 14, 2010 Discovery Demands
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 18, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT ECOLAB INC.'S Motion For Protective Order Regarding The Plaintiff's Oct. 14, 2010 Discovery Demands. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S OCTOBER 14, 2010 DISCOVERY DEMANDS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS 60 DAYS FROM THE INITIAL DATE RESPONSES WERE DUE TO RESPOND. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ICARD et al VS. ECOLAB, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories(Set One); And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Actual Production Of Documents To Responses To Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 18, 2011, line 3, PLAINTIFF JAMES ICARD'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One); And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Actual Production Of Documents To Responses To Request For Sanctions. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: SPECIAL INTERROGATORY #11 - GRANTED IF DEFENDANT KNOWS THE NUMBER OF HOURS WORKED BY PLAINTIFF DURING HIS EMPLOYMENT IT MUST STATE THAT REGARDLESS OF WHAT DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PROVIDED. SPECIAL INTERROGATORY #12 - GRANTED IF DEFENDANT KNOWS THE TIME OF DAY OR NIGHT THAT PLAINTIFF TOOK A 30 MINUTE MEAL BREAK IT SHOULD STATE THE ANSWER. REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS 20-22, 24-26, 29-30 GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE CASE NAME, CASE NUMBER, COPY OF COMPLAINTS AND SETTLEMENT DOCUMENTS. PROPOUNDED DISCOVERY IN THOSE CASES NEED NOT BE PROVIDED AT THIS TIME. REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENT 34 GRANTED IN SO FAR AS DEFENDANT HAS PRIVACY CONCERNS OF CURRENT OR FORMER EMPLOYEES, THE PARTIES SHOULD MEET AND CONFER ON A CONFIDENTIALITY STIPULATION, AND ORDER. IF THEY CANNOT AGREE ON A STIPULATION AND ORDER, THE COURT WILL DETERMINE A FINAL ORDER AFTER HEARING FROM BOTH PARTIES. DEFENDANT TO PAY PLAINTIFF SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF 1,220.00. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. ELIABETH LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 18, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT ELIABETH LEE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. AS TO THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING NEGLIGENCE OR SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN GIVEN TWO OPPORTUNITIES TO CORRECT THE SAME DEFICIENCIES IN HIS COMPLAINT AND HAS FAILED TO DO SO. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DANILO MOLIERI VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABLILIZATION AND et al
